## Description

@Wumpf

Wumpf

opened on Jul 12, 2025

Issue body actions [...] @Wumpf

Wumpf

opened on Jul 12, 2025

Issue body actions

Safari 18.5 (and lower) has WebGPU as an opt-in feature. Due to a (meanwhile fixed) bug in WebKit we're always defaulting to GL device on Safari (but you can still force it) even when Safari's experimental WebGPU support is enabled.

The bug has been fixed meanwhile, see

 Investigate `re_renderer` failure with Safari opt-in WebGPU support #8559

I have confirmed that Rerun works fine on WebGPU in the Safari Technology Preview 223 (WebKit 20622.1.18.11.5).

We should monitor further development and enable WebGPU in new enough Safari versions once we confirmed that a stable build of Safari works with Rerun @ WebGPU.

Reactions are currently unavailable

results.title: Dust 2: Frustrating, Broken and Iconic ⇒ Pley.gg
results.content:
### Pley Admin

Pley Admin is the official publishing profile for Pley.gg, used for articles, updates, and general site communications. Content published follows our editorial standards and is maintained for clarity and consistency across our site.

SPORTZINO Banner
Dust 2: Frustrating, Broken and Iconic

Pley is your all-in-one hub for competitive shooters. Get the latest news, tournament coverage, expert guides, player profiles, and betting insights across Counter-Strike 2, Valorant, and Apex Legends. Whether you’re following the pro scene or looking to improve your own gameplay, Pley delivers accurate updates, tactical tips, and exclusive content to keep you informed and ahead. [...] Despite the pro-players wishes to remove the map from the professional map-pool, statistics show that the map was played the most over the past 12 months at big events between the top 20 teams. It was played a total of 16 times with Nuke and Inferno settling at second place, having been played 11 times. This shows just how frustrating it must be for some teams to play on Dust 2, but also how immortal and an integrated part of the Counter-Strike scene, the map has grown to become.

Watch and learn more about Dust 2’s history as well as its best and worst moments here:

Dust 2: Frustrating, Broken and Iconic

The first version of Dust was released in Counter-Strike on the 5th. November 1999 and was called de\_dust. The first version of the map was not made by a map developer or even a Valve employee, but instead, a 16-year-old boy who stumbled upon the tools to make what was soon to become the beginning of Counter-Strikes, arguably, most iconic map ever.
